title: Actinocephalus armeria
intro: Actinocephalus armeria is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Eriocaulaceae, commonly known as the pipewort family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its leaves and stems are reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Actinocephalus armeria include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to promote wound healing and skin health.
taxonomy: Actinocephalus armeria is classified within the family Eriocaulaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the pipewort family. The Eriocaulaceae family is part of the order Poales, a group of monocotyledonous flowering plants. The Poales order is also known as the commelinid monocots. These classifications place Actinocephalus armeria within a broader group of flowering plants that share similar characteristics.
